questions.title: Adil — Consultation Liaison
questions.clinicalTheme: Treatment refusal, capacity assessment, opioid dependence, Afghan refugee
questions.pressureSequence: Refusal → capacity assessment → discharge planning → ethics of discharge to foreseeable harm
questions.finalDilemma: Ethics of discharging vulnerable patient to homelessness and heroin relapse

questions.title: Danica — Perinatal Psychiatry
questions.clinicalTheme: Bipolar disorder in pregnancy, valproate embryopathy, medication ethics
questions.pressureSequence: Assessment → medication ethics → fetal harm disclosure → competing duties autonomy vs beneficence
questions.finalDilemma: Maternal autonomy vs fetal welfare when patient has full capacity

questions.title: Hypnotics Quality Improvement
questions.clinicalTheme: QI audit, hypnotic prescribing reduction, non-pharmacological sleep strategies
questions.pressureSequence: Implementation planning → physical health monitoring → QI metrics → non-compliant consultant management
questions.finalDilemma: Governance escalation for senior clinician non-compliance with evidence-based protocol

questions.title: Aaliyah — Postpartum Psychosis, Cultural Safety and System Failure
questions.clinicalTheme: Postpartum psychosis with hypertensive crisis in an Aboriginal woman; tests capacity assessment, treatment refusal, Aboriginal cultural safety in discharge decisions, competing ethical tensions including alliance vs compulsory care, and system escalation when no mother-baby unit bed is available.
questions.pressureSequence: Medical and psychiatric risk assessment with organic exclusion → capacity optimisation and treatment refusal with delusional belief driving refusal → three-way ethical conflict between cultural autonomy, family care offer, and infant safety escalated by active nursery removal attempt → system failure with no MBU, adult unit refusing baby, and bed manager coercion to discharge
questions.finalDilemma: Safe disposition for a postpartum psychosis patient when no mother-baby unit is available, the adult unit will not accommodate the infant, and a junior registrar proposes AMA documentation for a patient who likely lacks capacity

questions.title: Mason — Inpatient Near-Miss, Observation Failure and Governance
questions.clinicalTheme: Near-miss inpatient suicide attempt following unsafe observation reduction during a short-staffed shift; tests immediate consultant response, post-incident risk and legal status management, competing governance and bed-management pressures, and M&M preparation with complaint response.
questions.pressureSequence: Near-miss discovery with immediate safety response → patient anger, denial, and request to leave plus farewell message collateral → systemic failures revealed with bed manager transfer pressure → complaint, distressed registrar, and M&M summary required simultaneously
questions.finalDilemma: Providing a factual M&M summary, responding to a formal complaint, supporting a distressed registrar, and implementing governance escalation simultaneously after a near-miss that involved a policy breach during a short-staffed shift

questions.title: Nina — Adolescent Trauma, Competing Duties and Post-Overdose Disposition
questions.clinicalTheme: CAMHS assessment of a 15-year-old sexual assault witness with daily suicidal ideation, self-harm, and cannabis use; tests adolescent confidentiality, Gillick competence, competing duties to family, school, and justice system, and post-overdose disposition without adolescent beds.
questions.pressureSequence: First assessment with confidentiality demand and suicidal ideation → engagement crisis with multiple contact refusals and disengagement threat → simultaneous duties to mother, school, and justice system in a single session → impulsive overdose with no adolescent bed and conditional safety plan
questions.finalDilemma: Post-overdose disposition for a 15-year-old with no adolescent beds available, an inadequate conditional safety plan, and an active court hearing as an imminent precipitating stressor

questions.title: Hemi Thompson — Delirium, Parkinson's Polypharmacy and Adverse Medication Event
questions.clinicalTheme: CL assessment of delirium in a Māori elder with Parkinson's disease and polypharmacy; tests assessment prioritisation with sedation request, delirium management with multiple pharmacological contraindications including haloperidol and promethazine, discharge capacity with whānau involvement, and open disclosure with root-cause analysis after an adverse medication event.
questions.pressureSequence: Delirium assessment with sedation pressure and multiple prescribing contraindications → complex non-pharmacological and pharmacological management with Parkinson's and QTc constraints → contested discharge decision between whānau home care and residential respite → haloperidol administered via verbal order causes aspiration requiring open disclosure and governance response
questions.finalDilemma: Open disclosure to the daughter and root-cause analysis after haloperidol was administered despite documented Parkinson's disease and QTc prolongation, causing rigidity and aspiration risk, via a verbal order from a covering doctor who had not read the CL note

questions.title: Mika — Aboriginal Adolescent, Psychosis Risk and Cultural Re-engagement
questions.clinicalTheme: CAMHS and ED assessment of a 15-year-old Aboriginal boy with possible psychosis, cannabis use, online threat and cultural mistrust; tests cultural formulation of the deceased uncle's voice, competing duties around refusal and third-party protection, and consultant re-engagement strategy after disengagement with refusal of a no-risk certificate to the school.
questions.pressureSequence: Diagnostic assessment requiring cultural differentiation of voice hearing before psychosis diagnosis → management of refusal, risk to named peer, and information-sharing under police pressure → disengagement with missed appointments, school demanding no-risk certificate, and registrar suggesting case closure
questions.finalDilemma: Refusing the school's no-risk certificate demand, preventing premature case closure by the registrar, and orchestrating assertive re-engagement through the Aboriginal health worker after the family feels blamed

questions.title: Amara — Seclusion, Trauma-Informed Care and Governance
questions.clinicalTheme: Acute inpatient mania with psychotic features in a woman with childhood sexual trauma; tests trauma-informed rapid tranquillisation decision-making, unsafe seclusion governance with camera failure and uncredentialed observer, dignity in seclusion including menstrual care, and consultant leadership under institutional pressure with retrospective documentation disclosure.
questions.pressureSequence: Acute mania with unsafe staffing and registrar requesting rapid tranquillisation → seclusion initiated with camera failure, uncredentialed observer, and dignity failure → morning governance crisis with sister complaint, NUM minimisation, and registrar disclosure of routine retrospective documentation
questions.finalDilemma: Responding to a complaint about undignified seclusion while simultaneously managing a registrar's disclosure of routine retrospective documentation and resisting a NUM's pressure to minimise a systemic governance failure caused by repeated executive staffing refusals

questions.title: Dr Ethan K — Impaired Practitioner, Opioid Dependence and Professional Certification
questions.clinicalTheme: Private psychiatric assessment of an impaired emergency physician with opioid dependence from multiple prescribers and depression; tests dual role as treating doctor and public safety professional, confidentiality limits when third-party information reveals child safety and patient safety risks, and resistance to institutional and personal pressure to falsify fitness-for-work certification.
questions.pressureSequence: Initial assessment with confidentiality request and active rostered clinical duties → third-party safety information from partner revealing drowsy driving with children and medication errors → admission with false certification request and CEO pressure to conceal
questions.finalDilemma: Declining to write a misleading fitness certificate for an impaired doctor who cannot afford leave, while simultaneously resisting an informal CEO request for discretion and documenting both as governance concerns

questions.title: Nadia — Syrian Refugee, CAMHS, Family Conflict and Online Exploitation
questions.clinicalTheme: CAMHS assessment of a 13-year-old Syrian refugee with panic, self-harm, and online sexual exploitation by a male relative overseas; tests culturally sensitive first assessment with interpreter, confidentiality limits and collaborative child protection disclosure, and collaborative management when father refuses further appointments and demands religious healing instead.
questions.pressureSequence: First assessment with father shame concern, silent mother, and Nadia's confidentiality request → individual disclosure of online sexual exploitation by adult male relative with fear of parental blame → school safety certificate demand, premature trauma therapy request, father refusal, and religious healer preference
questions.finalDilemma: Declining the school's safety certificate request, redirecting the school counsellor away from premature trauma therapy, and maintaining CAMHS engagement after father refuses further appointments in favour of religious healing while a child protection concern remains active

questions.title: Jane — Postpartum Bipolar Psychosis, Treatment and Placement
questions.clinicalTheme: Perinatal psychiatry management of postpartum manic psychosis after lithium cessation in bipolar I disorder; tests risk formulation with infant safety, treatment planning including breastfeeding pharmacology, lithium adverse effect management, and ECT psychoeducation, and competing placement decisions when paranoia escalates in a mother-baby unit without HDU capacity.
questions.pressureSequence: Postpartum psychosis formulation with infant safety and breastfeeding medication belief → treatment planning with lithium refusal, ECT psychoeducation, and obstetric comorbidity → paranoia escalation with oral medication refusal, partner requesting baby removal, and no HDU capacity
questions.finalDilemma: Deciding whether to separate mother and infant when paranoia escalates and the mother-baby unit has no HDU capacity, while managing partner distress, non-consensual treatment authority, and a system-level perinatal service gap

questions.title: Wiremu — Dialysis Refusal, Spiritual Belief and Capacitous Autonomy
questions.clinicalTheme: CL psychiatry assessment of a Māori man with schizophrenia and end-stage renal disease refusing dialysis based on spiritual belief and dream communication from his deceased father; tests culturally safe capacity assessment distinguishing spiritual experience from psychosis, ethics of forced dialysis when refusal may be capacitous, operationally realistic discharge planning with whānau supports, and open governance response after death including complaint management and registrar support.
questions.pressureSequence: Dialysis refusal with cultural-versus-psychotic distinction requiring Māori health assessment → acute hyperkalaemia with ECG changes and renal consultant demanding incompetence declaration → discharge to remote community after hui with carer burden and practical access barriers → death after missed dialysis with daughter complaint and registrar moral injury
questions.finalDilemma: Responding to a complaint that culture overrode medical care, supporting a distressed registrar who fears causing Wiremu's death, and conducting a governance review that examines systems rather than assigns individual blame after a Māori patient dies following a documented capacitous refusal of dialysis

questions.title: Youth Mental Health Service — Aboriginal Regional Area, Partnership and Safety
questions.clinicalTheme: Establishing a hybrid youth mental health service in a regional Aboriginal area; tests service model clarification before design, policy brief development using local data, inter-agency partnership conflict management balancing medical governance and community ownership, and patient-safety review after a 16-year-old Aboriginal girl overdoses following a referral pathway failure.
questions.pressureSequence: Service model clarification with competing hospital and ACCHO interests → policy brief using ED and disengagement data → partnership conflict with NGO, ACCHO, schools and community leaders → pathway failure resulting in Aboriginal adolescent overdose requiring simultaneous clinical response and system review
questions.finalDilemma: Responding to a 16-year-old Aboriginal girl's overdose after a referral pathway failure while simultaneously conducting a system review that identifies GP communication, NGO escalation threshold, and CAMHS discharge-across-communities gaps without attributing blame to individual partners

questions.title: Remote Youth Crisis — Telehealth, Workforce Risk and Pathway Failure
questions.clinicalTheme: Remote youth mental health crisis management and service governance; tests immediate telehealth risk triage for a suicidal 13-year-old, workforce and supervision recommendations for remote services with a junior registrar and nurse practitioner, managing competing expectations around local autonomy and specialist oversight, and incident review after a 14-year-old girl re-presents after self-harm following high-risk discharge to a cousin.
questions.pressureSequence: Immediate remote crisis triage with junior registrar and nurse practitioner requesting antidepressant → workforce and supervision recommendations for remote youth pathway → competing stakeholder expectations with resource constraint → 14-year-old re-presents after self-harm following high-risk discharge without adequate supervision
questions.finalDilemma: Conducting a systems review after a 14-year-old girl re-presents following self-harm after being discharged to her cousin because no bed or transport was available, while simultaneously supporting a distressed registrar who felt pressured to make the least bad decision

questions.title: ECT Service Audit, Change Management and Consumer Complaint
questions.clinicalTheme: ECT service audit design and governance leadership; tests clinically meaningful audit design under staff resistance, governance and consumer communication response to five identified process gaps, change management balancing urgency and professional autonomy with nursing safety intelligence, and open disclosure response to a formal consumer complaint about memory information, family exclusion and inpatient communication failure.
questions.pressureSequence: Audit design under senior psychiatrist resistance and workload framing → five audit findings with informal consumer complaints → change presentation with defensive senior staff, ignored nursing concerns, and consumer experience gap → formal consumer complaint requiring open disclosure and service strengthening
questions.finalDilemma: Responding to a formal consumer complaint about persistent memory problems and family exclusion while simultaneously advising the hospital executive, completing the change process, and feeding complaint themes back into the audit cycle without attributing premature individual blame

questions.title: Documentation, GP Liaison, Lithium Toxicity and Shared-Care Quality Assurance
questions.clinicalTheme: Community mental health team documentation and GP liaison quality assurance; tests just-culture problem assessment without premature blame, governance and process change recommendations including minimum standards and high-risk medication fail-safes, consultant response to lithium toxicity balancing accountability and psychological safety, and leading a shared-care meeting with GPs and consumers to develop sustainable shared-care boundaries after an adverse event.
questions.pressureSequence: GP complaints with multiple professional groups attributing blame to each other → audit findings of five documentation gaps with no agreed minimum standard → clinical service manager blame framing followed by lithium toxicity adverse outcome → GP liaison meeting with staff defensiveness, specialist-versus-GP responsibility debate and consumer medication gap
questions.finalDilemma: Leading a GP-staff-consumer meeting to develop a sustainable shared-care process after a lithium toxicity adverse outcome, while managing staff defensiveness, a consultant who believes GPs should take more monitoring responsibility, and a consumer representative who names patient medication understanding as an unaddressed gap

questions.title: Alex — Gender-Diverse Patient, ED Stigma and Complaint
questions.clinicalTheme: ED assessment of a 22-year-old transgender man with depression, self-harm and family conflict; tests gender-affirming formulation without pathologising identity, safety and confidentiality management with demanding parent, consultant response to ED staff misgendering and alleged discrimination, and complaint management after re-presentation following delayed help-seeking caused by previous discriminatory care.
questions.pressureSequence: Formulation with gender identity context, eating restriction and departure request → confidentiality management with partner collateral and demanding mother → ED staff misconduct with throughput pressure and junior nurse fear → formal complaint and more serious overdose with delayed presentation directly attributed to discrimination
questions.finalDilemma: Managing Alex's serious overdose re-presentation while simultaneously responding to a formal discrimination complaint, supporting a defensive ED team, and recommending service improvements — all while the investigation is incomplete and Alex attributes his delayed presentation to the original discrimination

questions.title: Donna — Acute Relapse, Discharge Barriers and Nurse–Social Worker Disagreement
questions.clinicalTheme: Acute inpatient management of a 39-year-old woman readmitted two weeks after discharge with alcohol relapse, suicidal ideation, renewed family violence exposure and homelessness; tests immediate risk and withdrawal assessment, management of nurse-social worker disagreement that has entered the therapeutic relationship, and consultant-level discharge decision under simultaneous clinical risk and bed pressure with only three nights of crisis accommodation available.
questions.pressureSequence: Immediate risk reassessment after sobriety with active family violence and housing barriers → nurse-social worker disagreement entering therapeutic relationship with Donna naming the conflict directly → consultant discharge decision under bed pressure with inadequate accommodation and unresolved family violence and alcohol risk
questions.finalDilemma: Making a defensible discharge decision when three nights of crisis accommodation is the only housing option, the nurse and social worker remain in disagreement, the bed manager is applying pressure, and alcohol relapse and family violence risk have not been specifically mitigated

questions.title: Sara — Community Care, NGO Collateral and Inter-Agency Conflict
questions.clinicalTheme: Community management of a 31-year-old woman with schizoaffective disorder, trauma history and repeated admissions; tests clinical and functional needs assessment at first community review, evaluation of ethically problematic pejorative NGO collateral used in risk plans, management of case manager and NGO support worker disagreement as a parallel process, and post-overdose risk management and inter-agency conflict resolution at a stakeholder meeting where NGO threatens withdrawal.
questions.pressureSequence: First community review with unrealistic support expectations and medication-abandonment link → pejorative NGO collateral entering risk plan without consent → case manager and NGO worker disagreement escalating to manager emails with opposing positions each containing valid clinical information → overdose night before stakeholder meeting with NGO withdrawal threat, housing risk and unnamed clinical responsibility
questions.finalDilemma: Managing Sara's post-overdose risk and disposition while simultaneously running a stakeholder meeting at which the NGO threatens to withdraw support, the housing worker warns of eviction, and Sara says she does not know who is responsible for her care

questions.title: Mia — Peer Worker Boundaries, Recovery-Oriented Care and Safety
questions.clinicalTheme: Community youth mental health management of a 19-year-old with emotional dysregulation, self-harm and trauma who develops a therapeutic attachment to a peer support worker; tests assessment of peer support role as protective versus risk, boundary and safety management while preserving engagement, nurse-peer worker conflict as a parallel process, and consultant response after overdose when peer worker was instructed not to respond to after-hours social media contact without an adequate crisis pathway being in place.
questions.pressureSequence: Peer support role assessment with nurse concern about dependency and personal disclosure → after-hours social media contact requiring boundary clarification while preserving engagement → nurse-peer worker conflict entering team with side-taking and Mia experiencing being fought over → overdose when Jordan did not reply following clinical instruction, with nurse calling for peer workers to be excluded from self-harm cases
questions.finalDilemma: Responding to Mia's overdose after Jordan followed a clinical boundary instruction without an adequate crisis pathway, while supporting a distressed peer worker who feels responsible, declining the nurse's conclusion that peer workers should not work with self-harm, and co-designing a service improvement response with lived experience input
